title: Structured State Space Model Dynamics and Parametrization for Spiking Neural Networks (SiLIF)
relevance_note: Closest competitor: SSM-inspired complex-state oscillatory spiking neuron; SPRiF must differentiate on slow/fast functional decomposition
quality_note: Strong insight; preprint only — lacks peer review; code available on GitHub

title: Balanced Resonate-and-Fire Neurons (BRF)
relevance_note: Single-state oscillatory neuron; no slow/fast split; important to cite and distinguish
quality_note: ICML peer-reviewed; strong experimental coverage; open-source code

title: PRF: Parallel Resonate and Fire Neuron for Long Sequence Learning in SNNs
relevance_note: Complex-domain oscillatory neuron; decoupled reset is computational not functional
quality_note: Strong parallelization contribution; ICLR 2025 submission; solid experimental benchmarks

title: Advancing Spatio-Temporal Processing in SNNs through Adaptation (AdLIF)
relevance_note: Adaptation mechanism; SOTA on event-based benchmarks; adaptation variable is not temporal memory
quality_note: Nature Comms = high credibility; thorough experimental validation; must-use baseline

title: SpikingSSMs: Learning Long Sequences with Sparse and Parallel Spiking State Space Models
relevance_note: Dendritic hierarchy + SSM; operates at network level not neuron level
quality_note: AAAI peer-reviewed; strong LRA results; 90% sparsity

title: FLAMES: A Hybrid Spiking-State Space Model for Adaptive Memory Retention
relevance_note: Spike-Aware HiPPO for rate-adaptive memory; not structural slow/fast separation
quality_note: Novel memory adaptation mechanism; preprint; good LRA benchmarks

title: Understanding the Functional Roles of Modelling Components in Spiking Neural Networks
relevance_note: Systematic ablation of LIF components; confirms reset is essential; useful background
quality_note: Journal publication; strong ablation methodology; limited to LIF analysis

title: Efficient Online Learning for Networks of Two-Compartment Spiking Neurons (TC-LIF)
quality_note: IJCNN = mid-tier; online learning focus; two-compartment concept partially overlaps

title: DA-LIF: Dual Adaptive Leaky Integrate-and-Fire Model for Deep SNNs
relevance_note: Dual spatial + temporal adaptation; adaptation is not state decomposition
quality_note: IEEE publication; dual adaptation is incremental over AdLIF

title: Parallel Spiking Neurons with High Efficiency and Ability to Learn Long-term Dependencies (PSN)
relevance_note: Eliminates reset for parallelization; validates interest in reset redesign
quality_note: NeurIPS = top venue; clean method; opposite philosophy to SPRiF

title: Unleashing Temporal Capacity of SNNs through Spatiotemporal Separation (STSep)
quality_note: Good video benchmark results; different level of abstraction from SPRiF

title: CRIMF: Channelwise Regional Integrate and Multiple Firing Neuron
relevance_note: Adds regional current state; motivation overlaps but mechanism differs
quality_note: IEEE publication; focuses on gradient/heterogeneity issues
